title: Nested NORN.md as synthetic rules
purpose: Subdirectories can have their own NORN.md files that provide directory-specific conventions. Rather than building a parallel activation system, these are registered as synthetic rules in the existing RuleEngine. This reuses trigger evaluation, presence tracking, and compaction recovery without new machinery.
task: Extend context/scanner.rs with a function that, given a PathChanged event, checks if the file's directory ancestry contains a NORN.md that hasn't been registered yet. When found, create a synthetic Rule with a PathGlob trigger matching the directory and descendants, register it in the RuleEngine via add_rule(). Track which directories have been scanned to avoid duplicate registration.

requirements.title: Nested NORN.md detection on PathChanged
requirements.spec: WHEN a RuntimeEvent::PathChanged occurs, the scanner SHALL check if any directory in the file's ancestry (up to the project root) contains a NORN.md file that has not yet been registered as a synthetic rule.
acceptance: Reading src/api/handler.rs checks src/api/ for NORN.md, then src/ for NORN.md
The check stops at the project root (cwd), does not walk above it
Directories already scanned are skipped (O(1) lookup via a HashSet)

requirements.title: Synthetic rule creation
requirements.spec: WHEN a NORN.md is found in a subdirectory, the scanner SHALL create a Rule with: ID 'norn-md:{relative-path}', a PathGlob trigger matching '{subdir}/**', DeliveryMode::SystemContextAppend, TriggerTiming::After, and the file content as the body.
acceptance: NORN.md in src/api/ creates a Rule with id 'norn-md:src/api'
The body is the full content of the NORN.md file

requirements.title: Single registration per directory
requirements.spec: A nested NORN.md SHALL be registered as a synthetic rule exactly once. Subsequent PathChanged events in the same directory or its descendants SHALL NOT re-register the rule.
acceptance: First PathChanged in src/api/ registers the synthetic rule
Second PathChanged in src/api/ does not create a duplicate rule
PathChanged in src/api/v2/ (subdirectory) does not re-register src/api/NORN.md

requirements.title: Compaction recovery via presence tracking
requirements.spec: Synthetic rules SHALL recover from compaction via the RuleEngine's existing presence tracking (RulePresenceSet). WHEN a synthetic rule is lost from context during compaction, it SHALL re-activate on the next PathChanged event in the matching directory.
acceptance: After compaction clears dynamic sections, the synthetic rule is no longer in the presence set
A subsequent PathChanged in the matching directory triggers re-injection via the rules engine
The re-injected content matches the original NORN.md content

requirements.title: RuleEngine integration
requirements.spec: Synthetic rules SHALL be added to the RuleEngine via the public add_rule() method. The scanner SHALL NOT modify RuleEngine internals.
acceptance: scanner calls engine.add_rule(rule) for each synthetic rule
The synthetic rule appears in engine.process_event() results when a matching PathChanged occurs
No modifications to rules/engine.rs internals are needed

boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the RuleEngine struct or its internal methods — use add_rule() only
SHALL NOT modify the Rule type or TriggerCondition type
SHALL NOT implement file watching — detection is reactive on PathChanged events
SHALL NOT handle always-on context files — that is NX-001
verification: cargo test -p norn -- context::scanner passes all tests
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
Synthetic rule IDs follow 'norn-md:{path}' convention in all tests
